vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1242: Board view follow-up — + New issue column footer button (deferred from PAN-1229)

plan.title: Board view follow-up — + New issue column footer button (deferred from PAN-1229)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The kanban board's column footers were designed to include a '+ New issue' button (PRD §4.7.6, `.add-btn` in board-opus.html), but PAN-1229 left this as a TODO at KanbanBoard.tsx:1928 because no backend endpoint exists to create an issue directly into a chosen status column. Users today must drop to GitHub/Linear to file work, then return to the dashboard — friction the redesign was specifically meant to remove. The frontend card layout, drag/drop, and merge-ready token swap are all shipped; this is the last piece of the redesign that delivers the inline-creation UX promised by the mockup.
narratives.Proposal: Ship the inline column-footer creation flow end-to-end. Add a new server endpoint `POST /api/issues` that accepts `{ projectKey, targetStatus, title, description? }`, resolves the project's tracker config, calls the existing `tracker.createIssue()` factory, and pins the new issue's dashboard column via the same `updateShadowState` overlay that drag-drop uses. Build a small `NewIssueDialog` (project picker auto-selected when the board is filtered to one project, title field required, optional description) and replace the TODO with the dashed `+ New issue` button styled per the mockup. Render the button only on `backlog` and `todo` columns to match the mockup spirit and avoid nonsensical 'create-an-issue-already-in-progress' states. Hide / disable for GitLab-only projects (tracker.createIssue is NotImplementedError). After creation, invalidate the relevant tracker cache, emit an `issue.statusChanged`-style domain event so live boards refresh, and rely on the existing `refreshDashboardState` flow to surface the new card.

autoDecisions.summary: Button shown only on `backlog` and `todo` columns, not on `in_progress`/`in_review`/`verifying_on_main`/`done`.
autoDecisions.rationale: Mockup at docs/design/mockups/board-opus.html:291 renders the `.add-btn` under the Backlog column only and does not repeat it on downstream columns. Creating a brand-new issue and immediately pinning it to `in_progress` / `in_review` / `verifying_on_main` is semantically wrong (no agent has worked on it yet); pinning to `done` would falsify the board. Backlog + Todo covers the user intent — file new work that hasn't started — and reviewer/work agent can widen the set if product disagrees.

autoDecisions.summary: New endpoint shape: `POST /api/issues` with JSON body `{ projectKey: string, targetStatus: 'backlog'|'todo', title: string, description?: string }` returning the created `Issue`.
autoDecisions.rationale: Mirrors the existing `/api/issues/:id/move-status` flag-driven design (validated targetStatus enum, server applies shadow-state overlay), uses the `projectKey` already exposed on `issue.project.id` so the frontend doesn't need new project-lookup endpoints, and stays minimal — title/description only, matching the dialog surface the mockup implies. Server-side validation rejects invalid targetStatus and unknown projects with 400; GitLab-only projects return 501 since `GitLabTracker.createIssue` is NotImplementedError.

autoDecisions.summary: Initial column placement applied via `updateShadowState`, identical to drag-drop.
autoDecisions.rationale: Trackers (GitHub/Linear/Rally) all create issues in their canonical 'open' state. The dashboard already overlays canonical→column mapping for any state divergence via `src/lib/shadow-state.ts`. Reusing it keeps the new endpoint stylistically aligned with `/api/issues/:id/move-status` and means we do not have to invent a second mechanism for 'create-into-column'.

autoDecisions.summary: Multi-project boards: dialog auto-selects the project when exactly one is filtered; otherwise shows a project picker populated from `useIssues().projects` (the same source the existing project filter uses).
autoDecisions.rationale: The kanban board is multi-tenant (selectedProjects can be empty=all, one, or many). Inferring the target from the filter is correct when unambiguous; falling back to a picker for ambiguous cases avoids a hidden write to the wrong repo. No new server endpoint needed — the project list already flows through `issue.project` on every existing issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Dialog uses Radix-based primitives already used by `ConfirmationDialog` / `PlanDialog`; no new modal framework.
autoDecisions.rationale: DialogProvider + ConfirmationDialog are the established pattern in `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/`. Building a third primitive is unjustified — the dialog has two inputs and a submit button.

autoDecisions.summary: No advanced fields in the dialog (labels, assignee, priority, team).
autoDecisions.rationale: Mockup shows a single `.add-btn` opening into a minimal creation surface; advanced options aren't in PRD §4.7.6. Labels/assignees/priority can be set after the issue lands via existing dashboard flows. Linear team and Rally project_oid are derived from project config, not user-chosen.

narrative.Action: In `src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts`, add a new `POST /api/issues` Effect HTTP route. Body schema: `{ projectKey: string; targetStatus: 'backlog'|'todo'; title: string; description?: string }`. Resolve the project from `loadProjectsConfig()`, build a tracker via `createTrackerFromConfig(...)`, call `tracker.createIssue({ title, description, team: project.linearTeam, ... })`, then call `updateShadowState(newIssue.id, 'open', 'dashboard-new-issue', targetStatus)` so the card lands in the column the user clicked. Invalidate the appropriate `issueDataService.invalidateTracker(...)` and append an `issue.statusChanged` (or `issue.created`) event to the EventStoreService. Return the normalized `Issue` payload. Return 400 for missing/invalid body, 404 for unknown projectKey, 501 with a clear message for projects whose tracker is `gitlab` (since `GitLabTracker.createIssue` returns `NotImplementedError`).

subItems.title: POST `/api/issues` with valid `{projectKey,'todo',title}` returns 200 and the created `Issue` JSON; the issue appears on the next `GET /api/issues` call in the `todo` column.
POST with missing `title`, missing `projectKey`, or invalid `targetStatus` (anything other than `backlog`/`todo`) returns 400 with a structured error.
POST with an unknown `projectKey` returns 404; POST against a project whose `tracker` is `gitlab` returns 501 with an explanatory message and does not call `createIssue`.

On success, `issueDataService.invalidateTracker(<projectTrackerType>)` is invoked and an `issue.statusChanged` (or `issue.created`) event is appended to `EventStoreService` so live dashboards refresh.
Endpoint uses `execAsync` / Effect / promise-based I/O only — no `execSync`, `readFileSync`, `writeFileSync`, or other event-loop-blocking calls (per CLAUDE.md no-blocking-calls rule).

narrative.Action: Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx`. Props: `{ isOpen, onClose, defaultProjectKey, targetStatus, onCreated }`. Reuses the Radix/ConfirmationDialog primitives. Fields: project picker (auto-selected from `defaultProjectKey`; shown only when more than one project is available; GitLab-only projects are excluded), required `title`, optional `description` textarea. Submit calls `POST /api/issues` with `{ projectKey, targetStatus, title, description? }`, then calls `onCreated(newIssue)` and `refreshDashboardState(queryClient)`. Show inline error if the response is non-2xx; show a loading state on the submit button while the request is in flight. ESC and the Cancel button dismiss without submitting.

subItems.title: Opening the dialog with `defaultProjectKey='panopticon-cli'` and `targetStatus='todo'` renders a title input, optional description textarea, a Create button (disabled while title is empty), and a Cancel button.
When more than one project is available, the dialog shows a project picker pre-selected to `defaultProjectKey`; GitLab-only projects are filtered out of the picker.
Submitting with a valid title POSTs `/api/issues`, calls `onCreated(newIssue)` once on success, and calls `refreshDashboardState(queryClient)` before closing.
A non-2xx response surfaces the server's error message inline (e.g., 'GitLab issue creation is not yet supported'); the dialog remains open so the user can retry or cancel.
ESC and Cancel close the dialog without firing the POST.

items.title: Replace KanbanBoard TODO with `+ New issue` column footer button

narrative.Action: In `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx` at the TODO marker (currently around line 1928), render a dashed `+ New issue` button styled to match `.add-btn` in `docs/design/mockups/board-opus.html:125-133` (transparent background, dashed border, centered plus icon + label, hover background = var(--accent)). Render the button only when `status === 'backlog'` or `status === 'todo'`. Click sets local state `{ newIssueDialog: { open: true, targetStatus: status, defaultProjectKey } }` and mounts the `NewIssueDialog`. `defaultProjectKey` is derived from `selectedProjects` (use it when exactly one is selected; otherwise pass undefined so the dialog shows the picker). On `onCreated`, call `refreshDashboardState(queryClient)` (already imported).

subItems.title: Backlog and Todo columns each render a `+ New issue` button at the bottom of their card list, matching the `.add-btn` style from the PRD mockup (dashed border, plus icon, muted text, hover state).
In Progress, In Review, Verifying, and Done columns do NOT render the button.
Clicking the button on the Backlog column opens `NewIssueDialog` with `targetStatus='backlog'`; clicking on the Todo column opens it with `targetStatus='todo'`.
When `selectedProjects.size === 1`, the dialog opens with `defaultProjectKey` set to that project; otherwise it opens with no default and shows the picker.
After a successful create, the new card appears on the targeted column on the next render (via `refreshDashboardState(queryClient)`).
The TODO comment `// TODO(PAN-1242): + New issue column footer button — see PRD §4.7.6` is removed from `KanbanBoard.tsx`.

items.title: Hide / disable button for GitLab-only project context

narrative.Action: When the kanban board is filtered to projects whose only tracker is `gitlab`, hide the `+ New issue` button (or render it disabled with a tooltip `Issue creation not supported for GitLab projects`). The check uses the same project list source as the picker. The button stays visible when any non-GitLab project is among the visible projects.

subItems.title: If `selectedProjects` contains only a GitLab-tracked project, the `+ New issue` button is hidden (or rendered disabled with explanatory tooltip).
If at least one non-GitLab project is visible (selected explicitly or by default), the button is rendered enabled.

narrative.Action: Add a Vitest suite alongside existing `issues.ts` route tests (or in `src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/issues.test.ts` if one exists, otherwise create it). Cover: happy path (creates issue, returns 200 + Issue, applies shadow-state), invalid body (missing title → 400; invalid targetStatus → 400), unknown projectKey → 404, GitLab project → 501 without calling `createIssue`. Mock the tracker factory and `updateShadowState`; do not hit real GitHub/Linear/Rally APIs. Verify the `issue.statusChanged`/`issue.created` event is appended to the mocked EventStoreService.

subItems.title: Test file runs under `npm test` and all new test cases pass.
At minimum the suite covers: happy path, missing title (400), invalid targetStatus (400), unknown projectKey (404), GitLab project (501).
Tracker `createIssue` is mocked — no real HTTP calls to GitHub/Linear/Rally are made.

items.title: Vitest coverage for column footer button + dialog wiring

narrative.Action: Add a frontend Vitest test (RTL) under `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/__tests__/` (or co-located) that mounts `KanbanBoard` with fixture issues across all six columns and asserts: (a) Backlog and Todo render the `+ New issue` button, (b) the other four columns do not, (c) clicking the button opens `NewIssueDialog` with the correct `targetStatus`, (d) submitting the dialog calls a mocked `fetch('/api/issues', { method: 'POST', ... })` with the expected body, (e) when only one project is visible it is pre-selected, (f) when multiple projects are visible the picker is shown.

subItems.title: Test asserts the button renders on Backlog and Todo and not on In Progress / In Review / Verifying / Done.
Clicking the button opens `NewIssueDialog` with `targetStatus` matching the column.
Submitting the dialog issues a `POST /api/issues` with `{ projectKey, targetStatus, title, description }` matching the user's input (verified against a mocked fetch).
Project-picker behavior: single-project fixture → picker hidden, project pre-selected; multi-project fixture → picker shown.
